dimensions.rationale: The supplied receipt SHA-256 matches the request, binds the exact baseline/candidate/tree, contains exactly 15 distinct zero-exit commands, and records LOCAL_SYNTHETIC_ONLY with no protected execution or remote writes.

The shared, delivery-status, and Stage 6 validation paths require the receipt6-bound v2 task and aggregate bundle for CLOSED, while retaining v1 evidence exclusively for PRE_CLOSURE. Candidate, tree, receipt digest, exact command subsets/full closure, local-only observations, and missing/stale/crossed evidence are checked fail closed.

Malformed, incomplete, non-PASS, or Git-drifted assurance remains pre-closure or invalid; protected and production observations remain zero/NOT_RUN. Structured provenance is scanned for credential and private-key patterns before deterministic hashes are excluded from entropy scanning.

Post-review mutations to effect-bearing workflows, runtime, schemas, dependencies, tests, evidence/status authorities, or assurance validators block closure. The reviewed run made no external writes and reports removal of ephemeral local outputs, so no external compensating rollback is required.

findings.finding: Candidate, request, model-family, task-ID, reply, and receipt bindings are mechanically represented and checked across the complete review history.
findings.resolution_ref: The provenance schema and validator enforce seven ordered attempts per family, fourteen distinct platform task IDs, exact historical identities and hashes, and two distinct final replies.

findings.finding: Local execution evidence is candidate-bound and preserves exact command identity, zero exit status, tool versions, sanitized output, and verified output digests.
findings.resolution_ref: Receipt6 has the requested SHA-256, exact subject, and exactly 15 unique zero-exit command records; the validators reject incomplete, duplicated, stale, leaking, or digest-inconsistent receipts.

findings.finding: Production-write authorization remains coupled to prospective capacity demand and Kill-controlled effect boundaries.
findings.resolution_ref: The retained implementation and tests require projected repository, LFS, object, and release-asset demand before effectful callbacks and preserve cumulative reservation behavior.

findings.finding: Post-review closure cannot silently accept mutation of reviewed effect, workflow, dependency, test, or assurance surfaces.
findings.resolution_ref: The protected-prefix set covers production workflows, runtime, schemas, dependencies, tests, evidence/status authorities, capture tools, and assurance validators; Git-bound validation blocks protected drift and dirty delivery worktrees.

findings.finding: Delivery-state schema alternatives no longer permit crossed pre-closure and closed facts.
findings.resolution_ref: The schema and regressions couple v1.0.4 to PRE_CLOSURE facts and v1.0.5 to CLOSED facts, rejecting mismatched cross-products.

findings.finding: CLOSED remains dependent on complete two-family provenance and clean Git-bound post-review integrity.
findings.resolution_ref: Only PASS assurance selects CLOSED, and independent status validation requires deterministic rebuilding, PASS provenance, exact candidate ancestry/tree, protected-path integrity, and a clean worktree.

findings.finding: Shared evidence validation routes Stage 6 v1 and v2 records only through their declared supported schema versions.
findings.resolution_ref: The explicit schema map preserves valid pre-closure v1 records, accepts candidate-bound v2 records, and rejects unsupported schema versions.

findings.finding: A v1.0.5 CLOSED status can no longer validate while Stage 6 remains backed by pre-closure v1 evidence.
findings.resolution_ref: PRE_CLOSURE requires all eight T0601-T0608 records to remain v1; CLOSED requires all eight to be receipt6-bound v2 plus a complete aggregate. Shared, status, and Stage 6 validators reject missing aggregate, stale receipt digest, crossed candidate/tree, or incomplete command closure.

limitations: This read-only review did not execute protected Oracles, real Gmail or private-repository operations, production workflows, deployment, publication, RMD-06, RMD-07, or final AC-033.

Receipt6 records local synthetic command results but retains no raw logs; repository hashes and platform task IDs are structural evidence whose independent platform identity still depends on the retained Codex thread audit log.

The available system Python was 3.9 and could not independently replay the Python 3.12 remediation suite; the review therefore relies on adversarial static inspection and the hash-bound Python 3.12 receipt rather than claiming an additional successful test run.
No production health or remote success is inferred from the local candidate, receipt, or deterministic post-review integration design.
summary: PASS: the immutable candidate and hash-bound receipt6 repair the Stage 6 evidence-coupling defect without weakening pre-closure behavior, secret safety, historical provenance, failure honesty, or protected post-review integrity; all findings in this gpt-5.6-sol seven-attempt history are resolved.
